ops.title: Modal .uv_sync() creates a pip-less uv venv, so a chained .pip_install() dies with \"No module named pip\" — use .uv_pip_install()
ops.body:
A Modal image that chains `modal.Image...uv_sync().pip_install(...)` **fails to build**: `.uv_sync()` produces a uv-managed venv (`/.uv/.venv`) with no `pip`, and `.pip_install()` runs `python -m pip install …`, so the build dies at that step:
```
/.uv/.venv/bin/python: No module named pip
container exit status: 1  ->  modal.exception.RemoteError: Image build ... failed
```
Fix: use `.uv_pip_install(...)` (present in modal 1.5.2) instead of `.pip_install(...)` after `.uv_sync()`, so the pinned packages install into the same uv venv.

Hit in athome `train_image()` (`athome/train/modal.py` ~219-228), which pins the TRL GPU stack via `.uv_sync().pip_install("trl==0.21.0", ...)` — the image never builds, blocking the whole Modal training backend (v0.4.0 blocker, surfaced by the live paid smoke 2026-07-14). The serve-side `image_recipe()` in `athome/modal.py` does NOT chain `.pip_install` after `.uv_sync`, so it's unaffected. Debug tip: the top-level `RemoteError` is opaque — reproduce the build alone under `with modal.enable_output():` to get the real per-step build log. Watch-out on the fix: verify `uv pip install torch==2.6.0` resolves the CUDA wheel the same as pip.

Relevant to the pending stream-trade U-MODAL and write-like-me trainer Modal migrations — check for the same `.uv_sync().pip_install` pattern there. Related: [[athome-ensure-write-auth-oauth-keyerror]] (the first blocker in the same smoke).
